description: Comprehensive monitoring dashboard for NVIDIA Jetson Orin devices using metrics from the jtop Prometheus exporter. Covers GPU utilization, unified memory, per-core CPU usage and frequency, thermal zones, power consumption breakdown, GPU/EMC frequency scaling, swap, fan speed, hardware engine status (DLA, NVENC, NVDEC), disk usage, and board info. Requires a jtop-based Prometheus exporter exposing jetson_* metrics.

panels.description: Overall GPU busy percentage reported by jtop. 0% means the GPU is idle, 100% means fully saturated. High sustained values may indicate a compute bottleneck.
panels.title: GPU Usage

panels.description: Overall CPU busy percentage averaged across all cores. On the Orin AGX this covers 12 Arm cores, on the Orin Nano 6 cores. A high average with individual cores at 100% may indicate a single-threaded bottleneck — check the per-core panel below.
panels.title: CPU Usage (avg)

panels.description: Thermal junction temperature — the hottest point on the SoC. This is the value NVIDIA's thermal throttling logic watches. The Orin throttles at ~97°C and shuts down at ~105°C. Sustained temps above 85°C suggest inadequate cooling.
panels.title: Tj (Max Temp)

panels.description: Total board power draw in watts as reported by the onboard INA power monitors. Includes all subsystems. Useful for tracking power budgets and verifying NVP power mode limits.
panels.title: Total Power

panels.description: Percentage of unified (shared) memory currently used by the GPU. On Jetson the GPU and CPU share the same physical RAM — there is no dedicated VRAM. High usage may cause OOM kills for GPU workloads or spill into swap.
panels.title: GPU Memory Used

panels.description: Current GPU clock frequency. The Jetson dynamically scales GPU frequency based on load and the active NVP power mode. A low frequency during high GPU usage may indicate power or thermal throttling.
panels.title: GPU Freq

panels.description: Whether jetson_clocks is enabled. Locks CPU/GPU/EMC to max frequency for sustained performance.
panels.title: jetson_clocks

panels.description: Active NVP power profile. Controls TDP, CPU/GPU clock limits, and number of active cores.
panels.title: NV Power Model

panels.description: Jetson board model and module identification.
panels.title: Board Info

panels.description: GPU utilization over time. Shows what percentage of GPU cycles are actively executing work. Useful for spotting inference/training load patterns and idle gaps.
panels.title: GPU Utilization

panels.description: Unified memory used vs total over time. On Jetson, GPU and CPU share the same physical DRAM — this shows the GPU's allocation within that shared pool. Watch for the used line approaching total, which can trigger OOM conditions.
panels.title: GPU / Unified Memory

panels.description: Per-core CPU utilization over time. Each line represents one Arm core. Helps identify single-threaded bottlenecks (one core pinned at 100% while others idle) vs evenly distributed parallel workloads.
panels.title: CPU Core Utilization

panels.description: Live gauge for each CPU core's current utilization. Provides an at-a-glance view of load distribution across all cores. Red indicates cores above 80% — useful for spotting hotspots.
panels.title: CPU Cores (Current)

panels.description: Per-core CPU clock frequency over time. The Jetson dynamically scales each core independently. Drops during load may indicate thermal or power throttling.
panels.title: CPU Core Frequency

panels.description: Temperature readings from all thermal zones over time: cpu, gpu, soc0/soc1/soc2, and tj (junction). The dashed red threshold line at 85°C marks the point where sustained operation may trigger thermal throttling. The tj zone is always the hottest and drives throttle decisions.
panels.title: Temperature by Zone

panels.description: Live gauge showing the current temperature of each thermal zone. Color gradient goes from yellow (warm) to red (hot). Useful for quick visual assessment of thermal headroom across all SoC components.
panels.title: Current Temperatures

panels.description: Stacked power consumption by power rail over time. Rails vary by board — the AGX Orin exposes VDD_CPU_CV, VDD_GPU_SOC, VDD_SOC and VDD_CV; the Orin Nano combines these into VDD_CPU_GPU_CV and VDD_SOC. Useful for identifying which subsystem is driving power draw.
panels.title: Power Breakdown (Stacked)

panels.description: Total module power draw over time in watts. Tracks the aggregate power budget as reported by the onboard INA power monitors. Compare against the configured NVP power mode limit to see how close you are to the cap.
panels.title: Total Board Power

panels.description: GPU clock frequency over time. The Jetson uses dynamic voltage and frequency scaling (DVFS) to adjust the GPU clock based on load and power constraints. Drops in frequency during high GPU usage indicate thermal or power throttling.
panels.title: GPU Frequency

panels.description: External Memory Controller (LPDDR5) clock frequency. Higher EMC frequency improves memory bandwidth. Scales dynamically with memory-intensive workloads.
panels.title: EMC Frequency

panels.description: Swap space used vs total over time. High swap usage on Jetson indicates memory pressure — GPU workloads sharing unified memory may be pushing the system into swap.
panels.title: Swap Usage

panels.description: Percentage of swap space currently in use.
panels.title: Swap Usage %

panels.description: Fan speed in revolutions per minute. Tracks cooling system response to thermal load.
panels.title: Fan RPM

panels.description: Fan PWM duty cycle percentage. 0% = fan off, 100% = full speed. Controlled by the Jetson thermal management daemon.
panels.title: Fan Duty Cycle

panels.description: Hardware accelerator engine frequencies (DLA, NVENC, NVDEC, etc.) over time. These engines handle dedicated workloads like deep learning inference and video encode/decode.
panels.title: Engine Frequency

panels.description: Online/offline status of hardware accelerator engines.
panels.title: Engine Status

panels.description: Root filesystem disk usage over time. Shows used and available space. Watch for used approaching total — Jetson boards with eMMC storage can fill up quickly with model files and logs.
panels.title: Disk Usage

panels.description: Percentage of root filesystem in use.
panels.title: Disk Usage %

panels.description: Available disk space on the root filesystem.
panels.title: Disk Free

title: Jetson Orin Exporter - jtop
